title: mesua lepidota var. parviflora
intro: Mesua lepidota var. parviflora, also known as the 'Kagola' or 'Mahua', is a small, deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Clusiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The health benefits of Mesua lepidota var. parviflora include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antihistaminic properties, which can help in managing allergies. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
